_cite.reason: 4c: relabel unlabeled workspace-analysis to labeled analysis (o5 §10); every binding sentence judged WORKSPACE_ANALYSIS

prior.content_md:
The workspace, as freight forwarder, has no registration or reporting obligations under MARPOL Annex VI.

The following reporting obligations fall on vessel operators and are noted for carrier qualification and Scope 3 data-gathering purposes:

| Obligation | Who reports | Where | Frequency | Relevant to workspace |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DCS fuel consumption | Vessel operator (>5,000 GT) | Flag state → GISIS | Annual | Yes — Scope 3 data source |
| CII rating | Vessel operator (>5,000 GT) | Flag state; published in GISIS Statement of Compliance | Annual | Yes — carrier qualification |
| EEXI confirmation of compliance | Vessel operator | Flag state | One-time | No — historical |
| IAPP Certificate | Vessel operator | Flag state | Survey cycle | No — compliance credential |
| Corrective action plan (D/E CII) | Vessel operator | Flag state (in SEEMP Part III) | As triggered | Yes — carrier qualification signal |

**Gap:** No centralised public portal was identified in the provided source pool that allows a freight forwarder to query CII ratings by vessel or carrier fleet in real time. Accessing this data requires direct carrier engagement or use of third-party vessel data platforms not represented in the source pool.

*Source: IMO EEXI and CII FAQ. Source: MEPC.385(81), IMO MEPC Resolutions Index.*
